mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change vs 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns in rising periods are not significantly above median baseline (Mann-Whitney p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6%, it signals a weakening labor market that depresses consumer spending and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as recession fears intensify.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of occurrences where UNRATE exceeds 6%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate create a stagflationary regime: rising inflation destroys purchasing power while tightening monetary policy raises discount rates, both compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the proportion of positive 20-day returns exceeds 50%, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero (two-tailed t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a recession, a rising unemployment rate prompts expectations of aggressive monetary and fiscal stimulus, which lifts equity prices as investors anticipate easier financial conditions despite weak economic data.
failure_condition: In NBER-dated recessions, the 5-day forward return following a 0.1pp or larger UNRATE increase is negative more often than positive (binomial test p < 0.10 for positive proportion ≤ 0.50).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP indicates robust aggregate demand and economic expansion, which drives corporate earnings growth and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the underlying trend persists.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 return is negative or below 0.10 over any 10-year period, or average return in rising GDP quarters is not above the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,000,000 units per month, it signals a contraction in the housing market that reduces consumer wealth and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as the drag on the economy materializes.
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1000, the share of positive 60-day S&P 500 returns exceeds 50% (binomial p > 0.50) or the mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer income and spending, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as aggregate demand rises.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, the median 20-day S&P 500 return is negative, or the proportion of positive returns is below 55% with statistical significance (one-tailed binomial p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, an increase in consumer price inflation compounds the hawkish policy response, raising real interest rates and lowering equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets rapidly reprice.
failure_condition: In periods identified as monetary tightening (consecutive FEDFUNDS hikes), the correlation between monthly CPI change and subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is not negative (rank IC >= 0) over any 3-year window, or the mean return in high-CPI months is positive.
